title: rosa × andegavensis var. schottiana
intro: Rosa × andegavensis var. schottiana, also known as the Alpine Rose or Schott's Alpine Rose, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a hybrid of Rosa rubiginosa and Rosa glabra. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa × andegavensis var. schottiana include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Rosa × andegavensis var. schottiana can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders.
